channel.title: Notes on errbufferoverfl
channel.description: Recent content in Notes on errbufferoverfl
channel.copyright: This content is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International unless otherwise specified.

item.title: Consensus and Humming in the IETF
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: This post summarises the key points of RFC 7282 an informational RFC (Request for Comment) published in 2014. This post is?

item.title: E2E Fear Campaign -- ACCCE & Police
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Post on Twitter Post on Mastodon 1/14 The ACCCE & police are currently running a fear campaign about end to end encryption centering their arguments this time on concerns about sextortion of teenagers.

item.title: Embodiment
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: The principles of embodiment look at diverse experiences and relationships as human beings embodying multiple identities and realities in “disembodied” online spaces.

item.title: Feminist Principles of the Internet
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: The Feminist Principles of the Internet are a series of statements that offer a gender and sexual rights lens on critical Internet-related rights.

item.title: surveillance capitalism
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Surveillance capitalism is a type of capitalism market form, and a technocratic-oriented phenomenon that centres on the commodification of personal data by corporations.

item.title: technocratic
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Technocracy is a form of government in which the decision-makers are selected based on their expertise in a given area of responsibility, particularly with regard to scientific or technical knowledge.

item.title: the state
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: The “state” is a centralised political organisation that imposes and enforces rules over a population within a territory. [!warning]

item.title: Autobase
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: An experimental module that’s used to rebase (follow?) multiple casually linked Hypercores into a single, linearized Hypercore for multi-user collaboration.

item.title: B-tree
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: A self-balancing tree data structure that is used to store data in a sorted and searchable manner.

item.title: DHT
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: A decentralized data structure that is used to store and retrieve data over a network. The HyperDHT is used to facilitate the discovery of peers and to enable connections between them.

item.title: DID
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Decentralized identifier (DIDs) identifies any subject (e.g., a person, organization, thing, data model, abstract entity, etc.) that the controller of the DID decides that it identifies.

item.title: Holepunch
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Holepunch is a company and suite of tools that “equips developers with a powerful suite of independent components to effortlessly construct peer-to-peer applications.

item.title: Hyperbee
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: An append-only b-tree running on Hypercore. It provides a key-value store API with mechanisms that can be used for: - Inserting and retrieving key-value pairs; - Atomic batch insertion; - Creation of sorted iterators.

item.title: Hypercore
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: A distributed, peer to peer, append-only log that can be used to create fast scalable applications without a backend.

item.title: HyperDHT
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: A dht powering hyperswarm, each server is bound to a unique key pair, where the client connects to the server using the servers public key.

item.title: Hyperdrive
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: A real-time distributed file system.

item.title: Hyperswarm
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: A high-level API for finding and connecting to peers who are interested in a “topic”.

item.title: IPFS
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) is a protocol, hypermedia and file sharing peer-to-peer network for storing and sharing data to a distributed file system.

item.title: ideas
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Technical Writing

item.title: the engineering anthology
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: This is a list of notable projects that I’ve finished and or currently maintaining. My (considerably longer) list of unfinished ideas can be found here.

item.title: The Inner Sanctum
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: The Inner Sanctum (2022), gouache, watercolor, and ink on paper, 100 x 70 centimeters, Colossal

item.title: The Offering
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: The Offering, gouache painting by Bill Mayer, Bill Mayer’s Personal Website

item.title: Aquarium Algae Scrubber
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: An algae scrubber is a water filtering device thats used to grow algae, which in the process, filters undesirable chemicals like nitrate, phosphate, nitrite, ammonia, ammonium from the water.

item.title: Ars moriendi
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Ars moriendi, woodblock 7 of 11, Circa 1460, illustrator unknown, en wikipedia. Source The Ars Moriendi (The Art of Dying) is a Mediaeval Christian death manual that appeared around the middle of the fifteenth century.

item.title: Coaster for Salut
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Coaster for Salute, gouache painting by @rowan_sugar, Coaster 3/6 for the Salut!, 2023

item.title: Lago d'Averno
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Lago d’Averno, gouache painting by Jacob Philipp Hackert, 1794.

item.title: The history of witches and wizards
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: The history of witches and wizards: giving a true account of all their tryals in England, Scotland, Swedeland, France, and New England; with their confession and condemnation, circa 1720, Collected from Bishop Hall, Bishop Morton, Sir Matthew Hale, etc.

item.title: Theatrum Chemicum Britannicum
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Theatrum Chemicum Britannicum, woodblock, circa 1652, Elias Ashmole, The Public Domain Review

item.title: Witches dancing in a circle with the devil
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: The history of witches and wizards: giving a true account of all their tryals in England, Scotland, Swedeland, France, and New England; with their confession and condemnation, circa 1720, Collected from Bishop Hall, Bishop Morton, Sir Matthew Hale, etc.

item.title: Witches flying on broomsticks
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: The history of witches and wizards: giving a true account of all their tryals in England, Scotland, Swedeland, France, and New England; with their confession and condemnation, circa 1720, Collected from Bishop Hall, Bishop Morton, Sir Matthew Hale, etc.

item.title: woodblock
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: A technique originally developed in China as a way of printing on textiles and later on paper, the oldest surviving fragments to survive date back to the Han dynasty (before 220).

item.title: gouache
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Gouache is a water based medium made of pigment, water and a binding agent, normally gum arabic or dextrin. Gouache is similar to watercolour in that it can be re-hydrated and it infuses with the paper.

item.title: errbufferoverfl
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: The easiest way to pronounce it is “error buffer overflow”… like the security vulnerability.

item.title: Wadawurrung and Dja Dja Wurrung People
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Wadawurrung Country Wadawurrung country encompasses the area from the Great Dividing Range of Ballarat, the coast from the Werribee River to Mangowak (Airey’s Inlet), including Djilang (Geelong), Ballaarat (Ballarat), Torquay, Jan Jook, the Bellarine Peninsula and Surf Coast.

item.title: What is a security engineer?
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: As a security engineer my main role is enabling engineering teams to make good decisions about how to build and maintain adequately secure systems.
